chunk_summary: Counselling strategies for picky eating and faltering growth focus on hunger rhythm, structured meals, limiting milk displacement, and promoting healthy feeding behaviors in children.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of hunger rhythm and structured mealtime practices in managing feeding difficulties.
Learn strategies to address milk and sweet drink displacement to improve solid food intake and texture progression.
Apply practical rules for mealtime structure, including fixed schedules, duration caps, and screen-free eating.
Recognize the role of family mealtime dynamics and behavioral approaches in improving feeding outcomes.
key_facts: Milk intake for children aged 1–5 years should generally not exceed 500–600 mL/day to prevent appetite suppression and dental caries risk.
Scheduled meals and snacks with no grazing help restore hunger–satiety cues and improve feeding behaviors.
Prolonged bottle feeding beyond 1 year increases risks of milk displacement and dental caries.
NICE guidelines recommend relaxed family meals, self-feeding encouragement, and avoiding punitive mealtime practices.
A typical meal duration cap is 20–30 minutes to prevent prolonged feeding sessions.
reference_quote: NICE recommends relaxed family meals, eating together, encouraging self-feeding, allowing mess, and avoiding prolonged or punitive mealtimes.
document_key_topics: Milk displacement and sweet drink management
